summary: Session 2 (final): pseudo-module semantics, the visible-module computation, and the compat matrix landed; the set is complete with no rendering change. computeVisibleModules (SessionSetsModel.ts) is the pure, exported, ordered module list the Set 092 renderer will consume: declared modules in manifest order (including declared-but-empty ones for Set 093's persistent children), one warning-flagged fallback group per undeclared stamped slug (alphabetical, never hidden), then the pseudo-module holding unstamped sets. Presence rule (routed ruling Q2 amended by the operator-confirmed Q8 matrix, which outranks it): pseudo appears iff unstamped sets exist OR the legacy root plan exists OR no other group is visible. Naming: Default when sole, Unassigned once any declared/fallback group coexists (a declared literal default slug forces Unassigned). Never-persist guard pinned with provenance-coupled tests across all three writer paths (pseudo-module authoring emits no module: field; only a picked manifest entry is ever stamped; hand-written undeclared module: default reads as a fallback group). Legacy root-plan mapping: LEGACY_ROOT_PLAN_REL always carried on the pseudo element; declared plans resolve through the new pure resolveModulePlanRelPath (degradation as data; the interactive modulePlanRelPath wrapper keeps the console warning). docs/planning/work-explorer-compat-matrix.md authored with every row citing its pinning test by name. Rendering byte-stable (computeVisibleModules exported, unconsumed; suite pins host+client). Suite: unit 1411 / pytest 2922+6 skipped / Playwright 20, all green. Verification: 5 rounds (gpt-5-6) — R1+R2 fixed in flight (matrix row-9 predicate, guard strength, empty-manifest x stamped rows 5a/5b, legacy-plan warning precedence), R3 disputed per the S1 calibration note and third-provider adjudicated minor-residual/dismissed (plan prompts carry no spec frontmatter by design; exact-string kernel applied), R4 purity Major fixed via the resolver/wrapper split, R5 VERIFIED zero findings. Advisory path-aware critique ran before close; its spec-contract finding was fixed in the close commit without re-running the raw artifact. No release: single boundary after Set 094 (verdict). Pending operator follow-through: author the locator/scope-check re-attach set.

adjudications.finding: s2-issues-round-3.json — 'declared-default guard remains unproven for the plan-import writer' (demands a module: default frontmatter pin on buildPlanningPrompt or end-to-end inspection of generated spec.md), rated Major by gpt-5-6
adjudications.third_opinion: s2-third-opinion-plan-import-stamp.json (gemini-pro): ADJUDICATED-MINOR-RESIDUAL, dismissed as resolved — the finding's premise misidentified the writer (buildPlanningPrompt authors plan documents and emits no SessionSet frontmatter by design, so the demanded pin cannot exist); the two frontmatter-emitting writers carry literal module: default pins coupled to picker provenance; the plan prompt's entire output surface is pinned exactly (verbatim scoping note, exact save destination, negative no-module: assertion); an end-to-end inspection of externally-AI-authored spec.md files is not automatable in-repo.

adjudications.finding: Advisory path-aware critique (path-aware-critique.json, produced via the sanctioned automated producer python -m ai_router.pull_critique): gemini-2.5-pro VERIFIED zero findings; gpt-5.4 ISSUES_FOUND, 1 Major contract-drift — spec.md S1 step 2 still said 'introduce a present-empty outcome', contradicting the S1 routed ruling and the shipped classification contract (no new union member; valid-empty = present with zero entries)
adjudications.third_opinion: Spec prose amended at close with a dated amendment note pointing at s1-empty-manifest-architecture.json; the template round-trip step's same phrasing fixed in the same pass (L-065-1 echo discipline); code, tests, and matrix already carried the correct contract. Per the iterative-dogfood convention (project-guidance, promoted L-070-1) the critique artifact stays as-written (raw, pre-fix evidence) and is NOT re-run: the fix is doc-only — no new code change — so the loop is converged; the authoritative VERIFIED comes from the cross-provider session verification (5 rounds, R5 zero findings), a different surface.
adjudications.confirming_round: s2-verification-round-5.md (session surface) + the doc-only spec amendment in the close commit
